entries.d: a big plant with a trunk and leaves
entries.s: The cat is sitting high up in the _____ on a strong branch.

entries.d: an animal that climbs trees and eats bananas
entries.s: The _____ with long arms is eating a banana high up in the tree.

entries.d: a colourful bird that can talk
entries.s: The colourful _____ can say 'hello' and 'goodbye' like a human.

entries.d: a very tall animal with a long neck
entries.s: The _____ with the very long neck is so tall it can eat leaves from high trees.

entries.d: it goes on tracks and carries people
entries.s: We took the _____ on the tracks from London to Manchester with 20 carriages.

entries.d: a black and white bird that cannot fly
entries.s: The black and white _____ cannot fly but swims in the cold icy water of Antarctica.

entries.d: a person who shows you around a place
entries.s: The friendly _____ at the zoo walked with us and told us about all the animals.

entries.d: a big wild cat, the king of animals
entries.s: The big _____ has a huge brown mane of hair around its head like a crown.

entries.d: very close to the side of something
entries.s: My house is right _____ the school, sharing a wall with it.

entries.d: inside something
entries.s: The new pencils are _____ the small wooden box with the lid closed.

entries.d: at the back of something
entries.s: The scared cat is hiding _____ the big sofa where nobody can see her.

entries.d: below something
entries.s: The ball rolled _____ the table and is now on the floor between the four legs.

entries.d: on top of something
entries.s: The open book is _____ the desk, lying flat on the top surface.

entries.d: before something, facing it
entries.s: The yellow school bus stopped _____ the school gate, facing the entrance.

entries.d: a place where you can see many animals
entries.s: We saw elephants, lions and monkeys in their cages at the _____.

entries.d: very nice to look at
entries.s: Look at the pink sunset over the sea! The view is so _____ I want to take a photo.

entries.d: not small, large
entries.s: An adult elephant is a very _____ animal — much larger than a car.

entries.d: asking about a place
entries.s: _____ is the bathroom, please? I can't find the door.

entries.d: not big, little
entries.s: A baby mouse is a very _____ animal. You can hold it in the palm of your hand.

entries.d: a person who is not a child
entries.s: One _____ ticket for someone over 18 costs fourteen euros at the cinema.

entries.d: a word to say where someone is
entries.s: The children are _____ school right now in their classroom until 1 pm.

entries.d: to carry something to a place
entries.s: Please _____ your packed lunch with you to school tomorrow for the trip.

entries.d: a word that shows a different idea
entries.s: I like cats _____ my brother likes dogs. We cannot agree.

entries.d: a pet animal that barks
entries.s: My pet _____ named Rex likes to play fetch and run fast in the park.

entries.d: your mother, father, brothers, and sisters
entries.s: My _____ has four people: my mum, my dad, my little sister, and me.

entries.d: you do not have to pay
entries.s: The water at the school drinking fountain is _____. You don't pay for it.

entries.d: your mother's or father's mother
entries.s: We visit _____ — my mum's mother — every Sunday for lunch at her house.

entries.d: some people together
entries.s: Our _____ has five children working on the same project together.

entries.d: not short, having a big length
entries.s: The snake has a very _____ body, about three metres from head to tail.

entries.d: a piece of paper that lets you go in
entries.s: You need a _____ to enter the zoo. You buy one at the entrance and show it at the gate.

entries.d: to wish for something
entries.s: I _____ to go swimming today because it is very hot outside. Can we?

entries.d: showing where someone comes from
entries.s: She is _____ Austria. She was born in Vienna and speaks German.

entries.d: twelve months, or a school grade
entries.s: I am ten _____ old. My next birthday is in June.

entries.d: a word for a boy or man
entries.s: _____ is my older brother. His name is Tom and he is 14.

entries.d: a word for a girl or woman
entries.s: _____ is my younger sister. Her name is Anna and she is 8.

entries.d: to say words to someone
entries.s: I like to _____ to my friends about football and video games during the break.

entries.d: a word for more than one person
entries.s: Tom and Anna are my friends. _____ always play together at break time.

entries.d: a word for me and other people
entries.s: _____ are all in class 1A together. There are twenty of us in the room.

entries.d: showing who gets something
entries.s: This birthday present is _____ you. I bought it at the toyshop yesterday.

entries.d: feeling good, not sad
entries.s: She is very _____ because it is her birthday party today and she has many presents.

entries.d: to allow someone to go outside
entries.s: The bird is stuck in the cage. Please open the door and _____ it _____ so it can fly free.

entries.d: a word for me and other people (object)
entries.s: The teacher told _____ a funny story. All twenty of us in the class listened and laughed.

entries.d: a vehicle with four wheels for driving
entries.s: Dad drives his red _____ with four wheels and a steering wheel to work every day.

entries.d: finally, after a long wait
entries.s: We waited at the bus stop for one hour in the cold! _____ the late bus finally came.

entries.d: that is very unusual
entries.s: Look — my old cat is swimming in the pool! _____ Cats usually hate water.

entries.d: I want to look at it
entries.s: You found something interesting in the garden? _____ Show it to me now!

entries.d: a hard piece of rock on the ground
entries.s: He picked up a small hard _____ from the ground and threw it into the water with a splash.
